disclaimer: v0.1 anchor crosswalk. NIST AI RMF function-level and OWASP item ids are high-confidence. MITRE ATLAS tactic names are high-confidence, but ATLAS technique ids (AML.Txxxx) are CANDIDATES to verify against the live ATLAS matrix. EU AI Act articles are coarse and need legal review. Each mapping carries a `confidence` field. Not yet authoritative — expert review pending (see taxonomy/README.md).

confidence_levels.high: directly stated by the frameworks or unambiguous
confidence_levels.medium: reasonable mapping, needs a reviewer to confirm
confidence_levels.candidate: plausible identifier/article, must be verified before citing

LLM01.name: Prompt Injection
LLM01.rationale: Jailbreaks and injected instructions bypass safety guardrails — a robustness/security failure.

LLM02.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure

technique_candidates: AML.T0024 Exfiltration via ML Inference API

LLM02.rationale: The model emits secrets/PII/proprietary data — a confidentiality failure.

LLM03.name: Supply Chain
LLM03.rationale: Third-party models/datasets/plugins introduce risk — governance and provenance of components.

LLM04.name: Data and Model Poisoning
LLM04.rationale: Manipulated training/embedding data introduces backdoors or degraded behavior.

LLM05.name: Improper Output Handling

mitre_atlas.note: Mostly a classic AppSec issue (XSS/SSRF/SQLi) downstream of the model; ATLAS coverage is thin.

LLM05.rationale: Downstream systems trust unvalidated model output, enabling injection attacks.

LLM06.name: Excessive Agency
LLM06.rationale: Too much autonomy/permission/tool access turns a bad output into real-world action.

LLM07.name: System Prompt Leakage
LLM07.rationale: Exposing the system prompt reveals guardrails/secrets an attacker can then bypass.

LLM08.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
LLM08.rationale: RAG/embedding weaknesses include retrieval poisoning and embedding inversion.

LLM09.name: Misinformation
LLM09.rationale: Hallucinated/false content that users over-trust — a reliability and transparency issue.

LLM10.name: Unbounded Consumption

technique_candidates: AML.T0024 Exfiltration via ML Inference API

LLM10.rationale: Uncontrolled querying causes denial of service, denial of wallet, or model extraction.
